title: True Few-Shot Learning with Language Models

structured.summary: This paper investigates the challenge of model selection in true few-shot learning, where no held-out validation data is available beyond a few training examples. It empirically demonstrates that standard model selection methods like cross-validation and minimum description length fail to reliably select good prompts or hyperparameters, leading to performance barely better than random selection and highlighting an overestimation of few-shot capabilities in prior work.

strengths.point: Clear formalization of true few-shot learning setting
strengths.evidence: The paper distinguishes between multi-distribution, tuned, and true few-shot learning, emphasizing the absence of large validation sets in the latter.

strengths.point: Comprehensive empirical evaluation across models and tasks
strengths.evidence: Experiments span multiple GPT-2 and GPT-3 variants, prompt selection on LAMA and classification tasks, and hyperparameter tuning on SuperGLUE with ADAPET.

strengths.point: Demonstration that common model selection methods fail in true few-shot
strengths.evidence: Results show CV and MDL barely outperform random selection and often select suboptimal prompts or hyperparameters.

strengths.point: Insightful critique of prior few-shot learning research
strengths.evidence: The paper argues that reliance on large validation sets inflates reported few-shot performance, calling for stricter evaluation protocols.

strengths.point: Provision of open-source code for reproducibility
strengths.evidence: The authors release code to facilitate further research and verification.

weaknesses.point: Lack of comparison to meta-learning or prompt-tuning methods addressing model selection
weaknesses.evidence: The paper does not evaluate baselines like MAML, Reptile, or meta-learned prompt tuning that might mitigate model selection issues.

weaknesses.point: Exclusive use of leave-one-out cross-validation without exploring alternatives
weaknesses.evidence: No justification or experiments with other CV variants (e.g., k-fold CV) or bootstrapping to potentially improve stability.

weaknesses.point: Limited exploration and ablation of MDL coding schemes
weaknesses.evidence: Only one MDL online coding approach is tested without investigating alternative compression models or coding strategies.

weaknesses.point: Insufficient statistical significance testing in evaluation
weaknesses.evidence: Reported results include means and standard deviations but lack formal significance tests to confirm differences.

weaknesses.point: Prompt selection baselines are relatively weak
weaknesses.evidence: Stronger heuristic baselines such as prompt ensembling, paraphrasing, or mining from external corpora are not included.

weaknesses.point: Hyperparameter selection experiments limited to ADAPET on SuperGLUE
weaknesses.evidence: No evaluation of other few-shot finetuning or prompt-based tuning methods to assess generality.

weaknesses.point: Reproducibility hindered by missing prompt templates and sampling details
weaknesses.evidence: Exact prompt templates and training example sampling procedures (e.g., seeds, stratification) are not provided.

weaknesses.point: Ambiguity in definition of true few-shot learning
weaknesses.evidence: Unclear whether unlabeled or unsupervised data is permitted, complicating interpretation of the setting.

weaknesses.point: Limited discussion of potential remedies or alternative approaches
weaknesses.evidence: No exploration of leveraging unlabeled data, task similarity, Bayesian priors, or meta-learning to improve model selection.

weaknesses.point: Experiments restricted to English and specific task types
weaknesses.evidence: No evaluation on other languages, modalities, or generation/structured prediction tasks.

weaknesses.point: Model diversity is limited to autoregressive GPT variants
weaknesses.evidence: No experiments with encoder-only (BERT) or encoder-decoder (T5) architectures.

weaknesses.point: Prompt transfer experiments are brief and limited
weaknesses.evidence: Only a few model pairs are tested, lacking systematic evaluation across diverse models and tasks.

weaknesses.point: Evaluation metrics focus solely on accuracy
weaknesses.evidence: No assessment of calibration, robustness, or uncertainty estimation which could inform model selection quality.
Section 3.2 lacks detailed description or pseudocode, limiting accessibility for readers unfamiliar with MDL.

questions.question: Can meta-learning or prompt-tuning methods with meta-learned initialization improve model selection in true few-shot settings?
questions.motivation: Such methods might overcome the limitations of CV and MDL by leveraging prior knowledge, potentially enabling reliable model selection without large validation sets.

questions.question: Would alternative cross-validation schemes or bootstrapping provide more stable and effective model selection than LOOCV on extremely small datasets?
questions.motivation: Exploring other CV variants could yield more robust estimates of generalization and improve selection reliability in true few-shot learning.

questions.question: How do the findings generalize to other model architectures, languages, and task types beyond English classification and factual probing?
questions.motivation: Understanding the scope of the model selection challenge is critical for broader applicability and for designing universally effective few-shot learning protocols.

questions.question: What role can unlabeled data, task similarity, or Bayesian priors play in addressing model selection failures in true few-shot learning?
questions.motivation: Incorporating additional information sources may be necessary to overcome fundamental limitations identified in pure true few-shot scenarios.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of comparison to meta-learning or prompt-tuning methods addressing model selection. The paper does not evaluate baselines like MAML, Reptile, or meta-learned prompt tuning that might mitigate model selection issues
critique_points.point_002: Exclusive use of leave-one-out cross-validation without exploring alternatives. No justification or experiments with other CV variants (e.g., k-fold CV) or bootstrapping to potentially improve stability
critique_points.point_003: Limited exploration and ablation of MDL coding schemes. Only one MDL online coding approach is tested without investigating alternative compression models or coding strategies
critique_points.point_004: Insufficient statistical significance testing in evaluation. Reported results include means and standard deviations but lack formal significance tests to confirm differences
critique_points.point_005: Prompt selection baselines are relatively weak. Stronger heuristic baselines such as prompt ensembling, paraphrasing, or mining from external corpora are not included
critique_points.point_006: Hyperparameter selection experiments limited to ADAPET on SuperGLUE. No evaluation of other few-shot finetuning or prompt-based tuning methods to assess generality
critique_points.point_007: Reproducibility hindered by missing prompt templates and sampling details. Exact prompt templates and training example sampling procedures (e.g., seeds, stratification) are not provided
critique_points.point_008: Ambiguity in definition of true few-shot learning. Unclear whether unlabeled or unsupervised data is permitted, complicating interpretation of the setting
critique_points.point_009: Limited discussion of potential remedies or alternative approaches. No exploration of leveraging unlabeled data, task similarity, Bayesian priors, or meta-learning to improve model selection
critique_points.point_010: Experiments restricted to English and specific task types. No evaluation on other languages, modalities, or generation/structured prediction tasks
critique_points.point_011: Model diversity is limited to autoregressive GPT variants. No experiments with encoder-only (BERT) or encoder-decoder (T5) architectures
critique_points.point_012: Prompt transfer experiments are brief and limited. Only a few model pairs are tested, lacking systematic evaluation across diverse models and tasks
critique_points.point_013: Evaluation metrics focus solely on accuracy. No assessment of calibration, robustness, or uncertainty estimation which could inform model selection quality
critique_points.point_014: Insufficient explanation of MDL implementation. Section 3.2 lacks detailed description or pseudocode, limiting accessibility for readers unfamiliar with MDL
